classifications.red: Established general principle or source result; do not claim novelty.
classifications.yellow: Application-specific derivation or synthesis not found at comparable specificity; describe cautiously.
classifications.green: Project-specific quantitative result not found in the audited corpus; broader review can still revise this status.

prior_art.boundary: Structural identifiability is established general theory.
Output-preserving parameter symmetries are established methodology.
Identifiable parameter combinations are established methodology.
Absorption strength and optical path length enter Beer-Lambert attenuation multiplicatively.
Thin-film optical inversion uses explicit constraints to recover optical constants and thickness.
Measurement combinations control optical-parameter and thickness recoverability.
Additional photometric information can permit unambiguous n, k, and d recovery.
Effective thickness is observation-model dependent and need not equal physical thickness.
Gap distributions producing near-exponential tails are prior art.
The nonparabolic intrinsic plus Urbach absorption branch is prior art.
Effective-thickness-dependent detector cutoff is prior art; the published thickness curve is calculated.
Finite-temperature carrier filling and free-carrier absorption in a degenerate HgCdTe specimen are prior art.

claims.claim: Structural identifiability differs from fitting precision.
claims.authorized: We apply structural-identifiability analysis to an HgCdTe forward model.
claims.prohibited: We introduce structural identifiability for spectroscopy.

claims.claim: Parameter symmetries and identifiable combinations govern recoverability.
claims.authorized: We derive the combinations for the declared HgCdTe operator.
claims.prohibited: We introduce parameter-symmetry or identifiable-combination methods.

claims.claim: Absorption amplitude and effective thickness enter the reduced single-pass response through A*d.
claims.authorized: The reduced operator inherits the standard optical-depth product degeneracy.
claims.prohibited: The amplitude-thickness degeneracy is a new physical discovery.

claims.claim: Eg0 and a uniform carrier translation enter the declared spectrum through Eg0+Delta.
claims.authorized: For the declared model, latent gap and carrier translation form one identifiable combination.
claims.prohibited: This is a new general theorem of spectroscopy.

claims.claim: The five-parameter unmarked spectrum has rank at most three and depends on Eg0+Delta, sigma_G, and A*d.
claims.authorized: We derive an application-specific rank bound for the declared distributed HgCdTe spectrum.
claims.prohibited: We prove unprecedented general structural-identifiability mathematics.

claims.claim: Two physically distinct parameter sets produce identical 281-point spectra when the three combinations are preserved.
claims.authorized: An exact model-specific counterexample demonstrates the practical consequence of the invariances.
claims.prohibited: All real HgCdTe spectra are non-unique.

claims.claim: A controlled carrier-sensitive marker raises rank to four but leaves one combined null vector.
claims.authorized: The diagnostic identifies which additional information breaks one null direction and which scale remains necessary.
claims.prohibited: The Dingrong free-carrier spectrum is proven to have this rank behavior.

claims.claim: Tail cutoff shifts logarithmically with effective-thickness ratio.
claims.authorized: Combining the Chang tail with the single-pass response gives the exact logarithmic shift.
claims.prohibited: We discover that detector cutoff depends on thickness.

claims.claim: Any number of tail-only Chang cutoffs has rank at most two for four nominal parameters.
claims.authorized: We derive a model-specific rank-two bound for tail-only HgCdTe cutoff datasets.
claims.prohibited: A new universal theorem for all detector cutoffs.

claims.claim: Mixed intrinsic and tail observations restore rank four with condition number 199.81 in the declared design.
claims.authorized: In the declared synthetic design, branch diversity restores local rank but not strong conditioning.
claims.prohibited: The condition number applies to real Chang specimens.

claims.claim: Changing only the fit window changes apparent tail energy by 60.1 percent for the same convolved spectrum.
claims.authorized: The source-conditioned computation quantifies fit-window dependence.
claims.prohibited: Herrmann's measured Urbach energy is wrong by 60.1 percent.

claims.claim: Dingrong's printed Eq. 2 and printed P miss Table 1 Fermi elevations by 11.297 meV RMS.
claims.authorized: A source-consistency audit exposes a printed-parameter mismatch; the row-implied P is diagnostic only.
claims.prohibited: The universal HgCdTe momentum matrix is 8.51e-8 eV cm.

claims.claim: No-crossing compositions censor the conditional critical-temperature distribution in the declared near-inversion model.
claims.authorized: The declared model exhibits root censoring and asymmetric conditional statistics.
claims.prohibited: The calculated fractions are measured topological phase fractions.

publication_framing.selected: HgCdTe-specific semiconductor optical metrology and inverse-problem methods paper
publication_framing.central_contribution: A source-grounded forward hierarchy that exposes exact identifiable combinations, quantifies operator-induced edge shifts, and prescribes independent measurements for latent-gap recovery.

limitations: The audit is targeted and can be revised by a closer source.
Theorem labels denote exact statements under declared assumptions, not unprecedented mathematics.
Yellow and green claims must retain their model and evidence qualifiers.
